name.fr: Ruchbrot (pain suisse à la farine Ruch)

description.en: Switzerland's everyday loaf: a lean, all-wheat hearth bread made from Ruchmehl, the darker, bran-flecked flour milled just short of wholemeal. A long, cold fermentation gives it a nutty, faintly tangy depth under a thick, chewy crust.
description.de: Das Alltagsbrot der Schweiz schlechthin: ein schlichtes Weizenbrot aus Ruchmehl, dem dunkleren, kleiehaltigen Mehl knapp unterhalb von Vollkorn. Lange, kühle Gare gibt ihm eine nussige, leicht säuerliche Tiefe unter einer kräftigen, zähen Kruste.
description.es: El pan cotidiano de Suiza por excelencia: un pan sencillo, cien por cien trigo, hecho con harina Ruch, más oscura y con algo de salvado, casi integral. Una larga fermentación en frío aporta un fondo a nuez, ligeramente ácido, bajo una corteza gruesa y masticable.
description.fr: Le pain du quotidien par excellence en Suisse : tout blé, à la farine Ruch, plus sombre, juste en deçà de la farine complète. Une longue fermentation au froid donne une profondeur de noisette, légèrement acidulée, sous une croûte épaisse et moelleuse.

origin.note: Ruchmehl is Switzerland's characteristic darker wheat flour, milled with more bran and germ left in than white flour but less than full wholemeal.

ingredients.note: Ruchmehl, split between an overnight pre-dough and the main dough

yield.shape: oblong hearth loaf with three diagonal slashes

keeping.note: A lean, all-wheat crumb; best within a few days once wrapped in linen or paper.

method.title: Build the pre-dough
method.summary: Mix a portion of the Ruchmehl with water and a little yeast. Ferment at room temperature for about 12 hours.

method.title: Mix the main dough
method.summary: Combine the pre-dough with the remaining Ruchmehl, water, salt and a touch more yeast. Knead until the dough passes the windowpane test.

method.title: Short warm rest with folds
method.summary: Let the dough rest about an hour at room temperature, folding it three times early on to build strength.

method.title: Long cold retard
method.summary: Refrigerate the dough for roughly 22 hours so flavor develops slowly.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Turn the cold dough out and shape it loosely into an oblong loaf.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Proof seam-up in a floured banneton for about an hour.

method.title: Score and bake
method.summary: Turn out, cut three angled slashes across the top, then bake hot with steam, dropping the oven temperature partway through, until the crust is thick and deeply colored.

source.author: Lutz Geißler
source.adaptation: Percentages taken from the site's own stated ratios; method paraphrased and condensed.
